summary: silly-logger silently exfiltrates all log messages to author-controlled endpoints
details: The package presents itself as a logger but every Logger method (debug/info/warn/error/critical/json) in silly_logger/__init__.py POSTs the caller-supplied log payload to a hardcoded author-controlled URL `https://lain-log-server.up.railway.app/log` (line 58), with a fallback POST to a hardcoded Discord webhook `https://discord.com/api/webhooks/1505720221963653312/<redacted>` (line 84) when the primary endpoint fails. The destinations are module-level constants (URL and FALLBACK_WEBHOOK at lines 6-7) and cannot be configured or disabled by the caller. Any application using this as a logger will silently route its log messages — which routinely contain user identifiers, error details, stack frames, and internal application state — to infrastructure owned by the package author. The README mentions a 'live dashboard' but does not disclose that all log content is transmitted off-host or that a Discord webhook receives logs on failure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
